sourceMovie.title: The Edge of Seventeen
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Edge of Seventeen (2016) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kelly Fremon Craig's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lady Bird
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Edge of Seventeen clicked for you, Lady Bird (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Edge of Seventeen?
faq.answer: Try Lady Bird, 10 Things I Hate About You, The Big Sick—each shares DNA with The Edge of Seventeen while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Edge of Seventeen?
faq.answer: The Edge of Seventeen (2016) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Edge of Seventeen worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
